creator: ohartmann@walstatt.org
text:
On Mon, 2013-04-01 at 16:05 +0200, Gerald Pfeifer wrote:
> On Tue, 1 Jan 2013, O. Hartmann wrote:
> >  Using the suggested change to the port's Makefile (port lang/gcc):
> >  
> >  Deleting --disable-bootstrap from CONFIGURE_ARGS results in being
> >  capable of building the port lang/gcc (with nothing set special to
> >  USE_GCC, so I suppose its either lang/gcc itself which compiles the port
> >  or with the use of LLVM/CLANG).
> 
> Thanks for looking into this.  Based on your findings I propose
> the following patch which makes bootstrapping an option.
> 
> That option is off by default, but you can generally set it on
> in your environment, and then you should be able to consistently
> build lang/gcc46 (regardless of what has been causing the failure).
> 
> Please let me know if you have tested this and how it went.
> 
> Thanks,
> Gerald
> 
> Index: Makefile
> ===================================================================
> --- Makefile	(revision 315611)
> +++ Makefile	(working copy)
> @@ -2,7 +2,7 @@
>  # $FreeBSD$
>  
>  PORTNAME=	gcc
> -PORTVERSION=	4.6.4.20130215
> +PORTVERSION=	4.6.4.20130329
>  CATEGORIES=	lang java
>  MASTER_SITES=	${MASTER_SITE_GCC}
>  MASTER_SITE_SUBDIR=	snapshots/${VERSIONSTRING}
> @@ -41,10 +41,12 @@
>  PATCH_WRKSRC=	${SRCDIR}
>  CONFIGURE_SCRIPT=	../${SRCDIR:S/${WRKDIR}\///}/configure
>  
> +OPTIONS_DEFINE=		BOOTSTRAP
>  OPTIONS_DEFINE_i386=	JAVA
>  OPTIONS_DEFINE_amd64=	JAVA
>  OPTIONS_DEFAULT_i386=	JAVA
>  OPTIONS_DEFAULT_amd64=	JAVA
> +BOOTSTRAP_DESC=		Build using a full bootstrap
>  
>  .include <bsd.port.pre.mk>
>  
> @@ -61,8 +63,10 @@
>  LIBEXEC=	${PREFIX}/libexec/gcc${SUFFIX}
>  GNU_CONFIGURE=	yes
>  CFLAGS+=	-I${LOCALBASE}/include
> -CONFIGURE_ARGS+=--disable-bootstrap \
> -		--disable-nls \
> +.if empty(PORT_OPTIONS:MBOOTSTRAP)
> +CONFIGURE_ARGS+=--disable-bootstrap
> +.endif
> +CONFIGURE_ARGS+=--disable-nls \
>  		--libdir=${TARGLIB} \
>  		--libexecdir=${LIBEXEC} \
>  		--program-suffix=${SUFFIX} \
> Index: distinfo
> ===================================================================
> --- distinfo	(revision 315611)
> +++ distinfo	(working copy)
> @@ -1,2 +1,2 @@
> -SHA256 (gcc-4.6-20130215.tar.bz2) = ba09fa687b5f55527952c11b588de359868a9257b7bf4271d1d575a441fd2f1a
> -SIZE (gcc-4.6-20130215.tar.bz2) = 68134979
> +SHA256 (gcc-4.6-20130329.tar.bz2) = 24af73abb791c569a45d5431669e91272cf71b88fae804fc6a12b3217bb328e9
> +SIZE (gcc-4.6-20130329.tar.bz2) = 68036750


In my case, the problem was caused by the option WITH_BSD_GREP
in /etc/src.conf (FBSD 10.0-CURRENT). Using the legacy GNU_GREP solved
the problem.
